source: /Users/jerod/refs/nd/opin/pdfs/2024/2024ND67.pdf page 181, where the word is line-wrapped as 'non-' at end of line and 'unanimous' at start of next line
evidence: The PDF shows the hyphenated word 'non-' at the end of line 181, continuing to 'unanimous' at the start of line 182, indicating a line-wrapped compound adjective. The DB incorrectly includes a space after the hyphen: 'non- unanimous'. Standard formatting normalizes this to 'non-unanimous' (no space after hyphen in a compound adjective).
note: The hyphenated word 'non-unanimous' is a single compound adjective modifying 'jury'. The space in 'non- ' appears to be an OCR/ingestion artifact from the line break in the PDF.
